heading: Mullumbimby Creek vs Mulyandry — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mullumbimby Creek, New South Wales and Mulyandry, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mullumbimby Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Mulyandry (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mullumbimby Creek or Mulyandry safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mullumbimby Creek has a flood risk score of 42/100, while Mulyandry scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mullumbimby Creek scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Mulyandry scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mullumbimby Creek or Mulyandry?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mullumbimby Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.13% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
